preferences: 2026-03-20: Don't list meetings I have declined in calendar briefs
2026-03-20: Apply meta-layer lens to emails — filter noise (newsletters, promotions, irrelevant gov bulletins, subscription confirmations for inactive services)
2026-03-20: Prefer brief responses without headers - use concise, conversational tone instead of structured sections
communication_style: Concise, actionable. Prefer bullet points for lists. Flag deadlines clearly.
meta_layer_lens: Apply judgment about what actually matters. Unread ≠ important. Filter: newsletters, marketing, promotions, government bulletins unrelated to my work (e.g. TPS for Burma), subscription confirmations for services I'm not actively using (e.g. VEED), social notifications. Focus on: direct messages, action-required items, opportunities aligned with my projects, deadlines.
notes: Add items via /remember or by telling Shiftshapr (e.g. 'remember: X', 'from now on: X').

ctas.name: Civic Mason badge

drafts_ready.name: Post-AI DOC event promo
drafts_ready.linkedin_meta_layer_post:
After the film, the real conversation begins.

**The AI Doc** asks the questions many of us are asking: What happens when the infrastructure of trust—what we see, what we believe—gets rebuilt by systems we don't yet know how to govern?

We're hosting small-group Zoom conversations to reflect on the film and explore what might come next. We'll touch on interface-level governance, synthetic content, and how coordination could work in AI-shaped spaces. Having seen the movie is not required.

Multiple sessions — pick one that fits. Scan the image or visit lu.ma/ai-doc to register.

#TheAIDoc #PostAIDoc #Apocaloptimist #AI
drafts_ready.linkedin_personal_quote: Quote/repost the Meta-Layer Initiative post from personal LinkedIn after posting it there first.
